summary:
The patient has eye opening to pain (E2). Due to being intubated, the verbal component scores 1 (V1); abnormal flexion results in a motor score of 3 (M3).

The GCS score is therefore E2V1M3. This aligns with standard practice where intubated patients are assigned a verbal score of 1 regardless of ability to speak, and abnormal flexion corresponds to a motor score of 3. Only choice C reflects these accurate assignments. 

Final answer: \boxed{C}
